issues.claim: Does not conflate traditions; respects sectarian differences
issues.objection: 7.22 sources Shankara (Advaita) and Ramanuja (Vishishtadvaita) with their own translators and copyright holders cited (Gambhirananda / Adidevananda). disagreements_among_translators field flags the specific point of doctrinal divergence with an explanation that does not flatten either reading. Engineering layer respects the convergence and tags STRETCHED where the verse exceeds operational scope (7.21-7.22's divine-agency claim, 7.24-7.26's metaphysical concealment, 7.29-7.30's chapter-8-forward-pointing material). The STRETCHED tag is honest for this verse: Verse claims Krishna is the actual granter (mayaiva vihitān); engineering analog operates at proximate-vs-upstream attribution scope (the framework is the vehicle; the customer-need-and-substrate is what actually grants the result). Counter-example carves out accidental success without disciplined practice.

notes: Engineering vocab does not erase Gita technical terms in 7.22: yoga-māyā / dvandva-moha / brahman / adhyātma / adhibhūta / adhidaiva / adhiyajña / prayāṇa-kāle / icchā-dveṣa preserved in engineering layer where present. PASS.
